commit.message:
fix: reliably read session in tRPC context using next/headers cookies()

Root cause: auth() uses createActionURL() which, without AUTH_URL set, falls
back to x-forwarded-proto header. In local dev this header is absent, so it
defaults to 'https' → useSecureCookies=true → looks for '__Secure-authjs.session-token'
cookie. But the login set an unscoped 'authjs.session-token' cookie (HTTP).
Cookie name/salt mismatch → JWE decryption fails → session null → UNAUTHORIZED.

Fix:
- Use cookies() from next/headers in createContext — guaranteed correct in
  Next.js route handlers regardless of request headers
- Try both cookie names (HTTP and HTTPS) so the same code works in dev and prod
- Add AUTH_URL=http://localhost:3000 to .env.local as the canonical base URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix waveform stuck at top: correct auto-scale initial values

irMin was initialized to 200000 but real finger IR readings are often
245000+, so irMin never got updated and the artificial floor compressed
all real signal variation to the very top of the display.

Initialize irMin=300000 (will drop on first reading) and irMax=0
(will rise on first reading) so auto-scaling tracks the actual signal.

commit.message:
fix: scanner ignores config ignore_patterns and ancestor .gitignore files

Two bugs fixed:

1. Ancestor .gitignore not loaded: When scan_paths are subdirectories
   (e.g. 'scripts', 'src'), the scanner only loaded .gitignore from
   the scan_path itself, missing the repo-root .gitignore. Added
   _load_ancestor_gitignores() to walk up to the .git root.

2. Config ignore_patterns not supported: ScanConfig had no field for
   config-level ignore patterns. Added ignore_patterns: list[str] to
   ScanConfig, compiled into a pathspec in FileSystemScanner.__init__,
   and applied independently of respect_gitignore.

Also updates DEFAULT_CONFIG template with common ignore defaults and
preserves ignore_patterns when --scan-path CLI override is used.

commit.message:
Add CI quality gate to block deployment on test/lint failure (#97)

Deployment had no quality gate — code could be deployed to Azure Static
Web Apps regardless of test or lint results.

## Workflow changes

- **New `test_and_quality` job** runs before deployment: installs root +
API deps, runs `npm run lint` and `npm test`
- **`build_and_deploy_job` gated via `needs: test_and_quality`** —
deployment is automatically skipped when any check fails
- Fixed `actions/checkout` from nonexistent `v6` → `v4`
- Added explicit `permissions` blocks to both jobs (principle of least
privilege)

```yaml
jobs:
  test_and_quality:
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - run: npm ci && cd api && npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm test

  build_and_deploy_job:
    needs: test_and_quality   # ← deployment blocked if gate fails
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
      statuses: write
```

> **Note:** `cd api && npm ci` is required — the root `npm ci` omits
Azure SDK deps needed by `api/tests/services/`, causing 2 suites to fail
without it.

## Docs

Added `documentation/ci-quality-gate.md`: gate behaviour, local
reproduction steps, and how to fix lint/test failures.

fix: set HOME=/data in Dockerfile so Copilot/Codex CLI can write their cache

When the container runs as a custom UID (e.g. Synology NAS `user: "1026:100"`),
the default HOME is `/` which is not writable. Copilot CLI writes to $HOME/.copilot
and fails with EACCES. Setting HOME=/data points it to the already-mounted,
writable data volume.

Auto-create GitHub issue on deployment failure (#96)

When the Azure Static Web Apps deployment job fails, contributors have
no automated signal — they must manually notice the failure and
reconstruct context from the Actions UI.

## Changes

- **New failure-handler step** added to `build_and_deploy_job` in the
Azure Static Web Apps CI/CD workflow
  - Guarded by `if: failure()` — only fires when a preceding step fails
- Uses `actions/github-script@v7` with the existing `GITHUB_TOKEN`; no
new secrets required
- **Issue created automatically** on failure with:
- `bug` label and standardised title: `🚨 Deployment failure on
\`<branch>\` (run <run_id>)`
  - Run ID, workflow name, commit SHA, branch, direct link to run logs
  - Contributor troubleshooting checklist
  - Assigned to `context.actor` (the committer/pusher)

```yaml
- name: Create issue on deployment failure
  if: failure()
  uses: actions/github-script@v7
  with:
    script: |
      await github.rest.issues.create({
        owner: repo.owner,
        repo:  repo.repo,
        title: `🚨 Deployment failure on \`${branch}\` (run ${runId})`,
        body,
        labels: ['bug'],
        assignees: [actor],
      });
```

Dependabot's existing `github-actions` update schedule will keep
`actions/github-script` current automatically.
